title: J'Meeb Gumb

{{Quote|Dirty scum used a skifter to rig the deck in a high-stakes sabacc match.|Description for the bounty on J'Meeb Gumb, from an anonymous originator|<ref name="SWBH" />}}
'''J'Meeb Gumb''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Guineo]] criminal living during the last [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. In [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], he was working in the [[Industrial District/Legends|Industrial District]] of the Republic's [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] at [[Haugg Nerf Industries]], a [[Death stick/Legends|death stick]] distributor operating under the guise of a [[Nerf/Legends|nerf]]-packaging plant. Around that [[Time/Legends|time]], Gumb had a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] placed on his head by an anonymous source for rigging the deck in a high-stakes match of [[Sabacc/Legends|sabacc]]. The [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]] claimed the bounty on Gumb when Fett was [[Infiltration of Haugg Nerf Industries|infiltrating]] Haugg Nerf Industries in search of information on [[Komari Vosa]], the leader of the [[Bando Gora/Legends|Bando Gora]], a secretive criminal organization.

==Biography==
A [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Guineo]] living during the waning [[Time/Legends|days]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], J'Meeb Gumb was a criminal<ref name="SWBH" /> as of the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]].<ref name="date">The events of ''[[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter]]'' take place in [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]] according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> At some point during that year, Gumb worked for [[Haugg Nerf Industries]], a [[Death stick/Legends|death stick]] distributor disguised as a [[Nerf/Legends|nerf]]-packaging plant, owned by an individual known as [[Groff Haugg]]. The facility was located in the [[Industrial District/Legends|Industrial District]] of the Republic's [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. While working at Haugg Nerf, Gumb received a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] on his head for rigging the deck in a high-stakes [[Sabacc/Legends|sabacc]] match by using a [[Skifter/Legends|skifter]]. An anonymous source offered a reward of 2,500 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] if Gumb was captured alive and 1,250 credits if he was simply [[Death/Legends|killed]]. The bounty listed Gumb's last known location as the [[Uscru Entertainment District/Legends|Coruscant Entertainment District]]. During the same year, the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]] [[Infiltration of Haugg Nerf Industries|infiltrated]] Haugg Nerf Industries while searching for information that would help him in his hunt for the five million credit bounty on [[Komari Vosa]], a former [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] and the leader of a secretive criminal cult known as the [[Bando Gora/Legends|Bando Gora]], which was rumored to be behind a large increase in death stick demand.<ref name="SWBH" />

{{Gamemechanics}}
During his infiltration of Haugg Nerf, Fett found Gumb<ref name="SWBH" /> near a control panel<ref name="BHSG" /> and captured him alive, claiming the bounty placed on the Guineo's head.<ref name="SWBH" />
{{Endgame}}

==Personality and traits==
[[File:Andthenasteptotheri-i-i-ight.jpg|thumb|left|120px|J'Meeb Gumb in Haugg Nerf Industries]]
Like many Guineo, J'Meeb Gumb had [[Hair/Legends|hairless]], [[Color/Legends|pale yellow]] skin,<ref name="SWBH" /> and stood over two [[Meter/Legends|meters]] in height.<ref name="BHSG" /> He was capable of rigging a sabacc match with the aid of a skifter, though doing so earned him a bounty on his head. Gumb also had some knowledge on how to use a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]].<ref name="SWBH" />

==Equipment==
During his time working for Haugg Nerf Industries, Gumb had a blaster that fired red [[Laser/Legends|energy bolts]]. He wore a yellow jumpsuit with a gray plate on its front and two silver-colored tanks attached to the back that each had a tube connecting them to the front of the suit. The jumpsuit he wore also had white stripes along the sleeves and around the collar. Gumb wore light-gray [[Glove/Legends|gloves]] and [[Boot/Legends|boots]] that were a darker gray. In addition, Gumb wore a pair of gray [[Goggles/Legends|goggles]].<ref name="SWBH" />

==Behind the scenes==
J'Meeb Gumb made his first appearance in ''[[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter]]'', a [[Star Wars video games|video game]] produced by [[LucasArts]] and released in [[December]] [[2002]].<ref name="SWBH" /> Before appearing in the game, he received an entry in ''[[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter: Prima's Official Strategy Guide]]'', the game's corresponding guide, which was published by [[Prima Games]] and released in [[November]] of the same year.<ref name="BHSG" /> In the game, Gumb is one of five bounties located in the Industrial District, which is the second level on the game's second world.<ref name="SWBH" />

In ''Bounty Hunter'', the player controls Jango Fett, and since claiming Gumb's bounty is not essential to the game, the player has several ways to go about doing so. The player can ignore Gumb entirely, kill him and not claim the bounty, kill him and claim the bounty, or capture him and claim the bounty. This article assumes that Fett captured Gumb alive, as doing so gives Fett the maximum reward possible.<ref name="SWBH" /> J'Meeb Gumb's name is similar to the fictional serial killer [[Wikipedia:Buffalo Bill (character)|Jame Gumb]], who was the primary antagonist of the [[1991]] film [[Wikipedia:The Silence of the Lambs (film)|''The Silence of the Lambs'']], which was based on a novel of the [[Wikipedia:The Silence of the Lambs|same name]].<ref name="TSOTL">{{WebCite|url=https://www.rollingstone.com/movies/movie-news/silence-of-the-lambs-at-25-the-complete-buffalo-bill-story-227700|text='Silence of the Lambs' at 25: The Complete Buffalo Bill Story|author=Grow, Kory|date=2016-02-14 2:00 PM ET|language=English|archivedate=20180811195956}}</ref>
